In Vice City, the only rule is don't get caught.
Sunsets are prettier when you're driving away from a crime scene.
Loyalty is expensive. Most people can't afford it.
Every palm tree has seen things it'll never tell.
The American Dream is just a getaway car with a full tank.
Neon never sleeps, and neither do we.
Money talks. In this town, it screams.
You can leave Vice City, but Vice City never leaves you.
Trust is a luxury. Cash is a necessity.
Some people chase the sunset. We chase the score.
The house always wins — unless you own the house.
Paradise has a price, and it's usually paid in full.
